title: allium preslianum
intro: Allium preslianum, also known as the wild garlic or field garlic, is a perennial plant species within the Allium genus, characterized by its strong onion-like smell and edible bulbs. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Allium preslianum include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Allium preslianum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
